newsapi.title: A California farmer is giving away tons of nectarines that he’s not allowed to sell - AP News

Thousands of visitors have flocked to Cesar Moras farm in central California this week to gather free nectarines.

bbc.title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
bbc.description: More than 165 million people are enduring dangerous heat and humidity, with temperatures expected to stay at 38C (100F) for several days.

bbc.title: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
bbc.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says the target is key infrastructure "that generates revenue for Russia's war".

bbc.title: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
bbc.description: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

bbc.title: Former chess champion Kramnik banned over cheating remarks
bbc.description: The former Russian world chess champion had accused other players, including the late Daniel Naroditsky, of cheating.

bbc.title: Injury ends Williams' Wimbledon comeback
bbc.description: Serena Williams pulls out of her planned Wimbledon doubles appearance with older sister Venus because of a knee injury.

bbc.title: Pope urges Europe to do more for migrants as he visits gateway island
bbc.description: The pontiff pays tribute to migrants who lost their lives at sea at a cemetery on the Italian island of Lampedusa.

bbc.title: Row after Indian state drops eggs from school lunch menu
bbc.description: West Bengal's move to replace eggs in some school meals has sparked a wider debate over nutrition and choice.

bbc.title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce in NYC ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
bbc.description: The star-studded celebration saw several streets in Midtown Manhattan shut, as hordes of fans gathered outside the arena.

bbc.title: 'Start work at 11' - but will other bosses be as flexible over England's 1am match?
bbc.description: Employers are being urged to use their "common sense" to allow staff to work flexibly where they can.

bbc.title: No-gift policy for Taylor Swift, but how much should you give at a wedding?
bbc.description: Wedding lists are being replaced by cash requests, but guests are divided over how much to give.

bbc.title: Tackle workplace sickness to unlock hidden growth, former John Lewis boss says
bbc.description: More than 250 of the UK's biggest employers have signed up to his 'Get Britain Working' taskforce.

bbc.title: Burnham says there is some room for movement on tax
bbc.description: But the likely next PM says he will stick to Labour's pledges to not raise VAT, income tax or national insurance.

bbc.title: Pubs allowed to stay open until 5am on Monday for England Mexico match
bbc.description: The government had initially said it would not relax licensing laws further for the World Cup.

bbc.title: AI is 'not smart' so what's next in artificial intelligence?
bbc.description: Leading AI researcher Yan LeCun has a start-up which is developing a more flexible AI system.

bbc.title: 'Less than 10% sheep': How millions may have unknowingly eaten goat, skin and fat kebabs
bbc.description: Millions are likely to have eaten "lamb" kebabs that were actually made with goat, skin and fat.

bbc.title: Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
bbc.description: The ads use terms including “rape” and “child video” and link to content on the messaging app Telegram.
